assessor_1_raw: The LTR vs. BM25 experiment is well-designed. Temporal splitting is appropriate, label construction is principled with acknowledged limitations, both models receive equivalent tuning, and the secondary metric suite (IPW NDCG, tail query NDCG, seller quality lift) addresses the main alternative explanations. The scope is appropriately restricted to offline evaluation. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: Strong experiment design for marketplace search ranking. The position-1/2 exclusion from training is a valid debiasing choice. IPW-adjusted NDCG addresses evaluation circularity. Cross-seller stratification and seller quality lift metrics directly test the hypothesis that semantic matching (not seller quality signals) drives improvement. The preprocessing fit-apply separation is correctly specified. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: This experiment design is methodologically sound. The 18-month temporal split is well-justified for the non-stationary search domain. Label construction via pairwise implicit feedback with purchase weighting is standard LTR practice. The baseline is not a strawman — it receives seller post-processing and equivalent tuning. NDCG@10 is the right metric. The stratified analyses comprehensively address alternative explanations. No methodological flaws. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: Three independent assessors unanimous: defense_wins. No dissenting views. The experiment design is consistently rated as methodologically sound with appropriate controls.
